text: ESI Level 1: Immediate life-saving intervention required. Cardiac arrest, respiratory failure, severe haemorrhage.
ESI Level 2: High-risk situation or acute changes. Chest pain with cardiac risk, severe dyspnoea, altered mental status.
ACS Red Flags: Crushing chest pain radiating to arm or jaw, diaphoresis, nausea, dyspnoea, syncope.
Pulmonary Embolism: Acute dyspnoea, pleuritic chest pain, haemoptysis, tachycardia, hypoxia.
Wells PE Score: DVT signs +3, PE likely +3, HR>100 +1.5, immobilisation +1.5, previous PE/DVT +1.5, haemoptysis +1, malignancy +1.
MONA ACS protocol: Morphine IV, Oxygen if SpO2<94%, Nitrates if BP>90, Aspirin 300mg loading.
Troponin: High-sensitivity troponin I or T. Elevated above 99th percentile. Serial at 0h and 1-3h.
Aortic dissection: Tearing back pain, unequal arm BPs. Avoid thrombolytics. Emergency CT angiography.
Tension pneumothorax: Absent breath sounds, tracheal deviation, hypotension. Immediate needle decompression.
Sepsis: SIRS criteria + infection. Lactate >2. Start antibiotics within 1 hour. IV fluids 30ml/kg.
Drug interaction: Warfarin + NSAIDs increases bleeding risk. Monitor INR. Warfarin + metronidazole potentiates anticoagulation.
Aspirin: Acetylsalicylic acid. Antiplatelet 75-100mg OD. ACS loading 300mg. Avoid in children.
